_meta.purpose: Remote-work tax residency checker (task 2), international mode — Phase 1 data. For a US citizen working remotely from abroad for a US company. The tool is a rules-map + explainer, not a calculator: which rules apply, what's owed where, what needs a professional. The FEIE math reuses the existing feie.ts engine and the 2026 FEIE amount in federal.ts.
_meta.phase: 1 — data, pending owner sign-off. Confirm treaty status against IRS Pub 901 and totalization against the SSA list before Phase 2.

universal_facts.us_citizens_taxed_worldwide: A US citizen owes US tax on worldwide income regardless of where they live or work.
universal_facts.feie_2026: Foreign Earned Income Exclusion for 2026 is $132,900 (from federal.ts, foreignEarnedIncomeExclusion.amount; re-confirm against the IRS Rev. Proc.). Requires either the Physical Presence test (330 full days in any 12 months abroad) or the Bona Fide Residence test.
universal_facts.ftc_vs_feie: Foreign Tax Credit and FEIE are alternative reliefs; FTC is usually better in high-tax countries, FEIE in low/no-tax countries. Not both on the same income.
universal_facts.employer_side: The part most content omits: an employee working from a country can create permanent-establishment and payroll-registration risk for the EMPLOYER — which is why 'can I just do it quietly' is the real question underneath.

_meta.schema: code -> { name, totalization_agreement (SSA), tax_treaty (IRS Pub 901 — pending), nomad_visa_note (pending), note, source_url, verify_status }

PT.name: Portugal
PT.nomad_visa_note: Portugal offers a digital-nomad / D8 visa; local tax treatment of remote income depends on residency days and the NHR successor regime — confirm current rules.
PT.note: Popular nomad destination; totalization agreement in force per SSA.

ES.name: Spain
ES.nomad_visa_note: Spain's digital-nomad visa (Beckham-law-adjacent regime) can offer a favorable flat rate — confirm eligibility and current rates.
ES.note: Totalization agreement in force per SSA.

IT.name: Italy
IT.nomad_visa_note: Italy launched a digital-nomad visa; impatriate regimes exist — confirm current terms.
IT.note: Totalization agreement in force per SSA.

DE.name: Germany
DE.nomad_visa_note: Freelance/self-employment visa routes exist; employee remote work raises employer PE questions.
DE.note: Totalization agreement in force per SSA.

FR.name: France
FR.nomad_visa_note: Long-stay visa routes; high local tax makes FTC usually better than FEIE.
FR.note: Totalization agreement in force per SSA.

GB.name: United Kingdom
GB.nomad_visa_note: No dedicated nomad visa; residency and the statutory residence test drive UK tax.
GB.note: Totalization agreement in force per SSA.

CA.name: Canada
CA.nomad_visa_note: No dedicated nomad visa; proximity makes day-count and residency ties central.
CA.note: Totalization agreement in force per SSA.

AU.name: Australia
AU.nomad_visa_note: No dedicated nomad visa; residency tests apply.
AU.note: Totalization agreement in force per SSA.

BR.name: Brazil
BR.nomad_visa_note: Brazil offers a digital-nomad visa. NOTE: the US has NO income tax treaty with Brazil (confirm) — FTC mechanics matter more here.
BR.note: Totalization agreement in force per SSA (one of 4 in the Americas); income tax treaty status must be confirmed and is believed absent.

CL.name: Chile
CL.nomad_visa_note: Confirm current nomad routes; US-Chile income tax treaty entered into force relatively recently — confirm.
CL.note: Totalization agreement in force per SSA.

MX.name: Mexico
MX.nomad_visa_note: Temporary resident visa is commonly used by nomads. NO totalization agreement — self-employment could face dual social-security exposure; confirm.
MX.note: NO totalization agreement per SSA. This is a key distinction for SE workers.

TH.name: Thailand
TH.nomad_visa_note: Thailand's long-term-resident and DTV nomad visas exist; local tax on remitted income changed recently — confirm.

AE.name: United Arab Emirates
AE.nomad_visa_note: UAE offers a remote-work visa; no personal income tax locally, so FEIE (not FTC) is usually the relevant relief.
AE.note: NO totalization agreement and (believed) no US income tax treaty — confirm both.

SG.name: Singapore
SG.nomad_visa_note: No dedicated nomad visa; work passes are employer-sponsored.
SG.note: NO totalization agreement and (believed) no US income tax treaty — confirm both.

ID.name: Indonesia
ID.nomad_visa_note: Indonesia (Bali) has introduced nomad-visa routes; confirm current local tax treatment.

_owner_review_notes: Totalization flags are from the SSA overview (authoritative list) but each country row should be checked against the SSA per-country page before shipping; the file source_url points there.

tax_treaty is deliberately left as 'pending_confirm_irs_pub901' for EVERY country — income tax treaty status must be confirmed against IRS Publication 901 / the IRS treaty table, not asserted. Brazil, UAE, and Singapore are believed to have NO US income tax treaty; confirm.

nomad_visa_note fields are the most volatile data on the site — nomad-visa tax rules change frequently. They ship only with a visible 'last verified' date and a 'confirm current rules' caution, or not at all.
Re-confirm the 2026 FEIE amount ($132,900 in federal.ts) against the IRS Rev. Proc. as part of sign-off.
The disclaimer posture here is the strongest on the site: 'this maps the rules; cross-border filing needs a professional.'
